sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI I.T.R.A. No. 284 to 286 of 2025 Date Order with Signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For order on office objection No.28 2. For order on CMA No.1599 /2025 (exemption) 3. For hearing of Main Case 29.01.2026 Mr. Abdul Basit holding brief for Dr. Shahnawaz Memon, advocate These matters are pending since 2025 without any progress, even notices have not been sought/issued till date. Diary sheet demonstrates that on each occasion, time was sought. On 22.01.2026, following order was passed:- “Dr. Imtiaz advocate holding brief for Dr. Shahnawaz Memon advocate for the applicant and requests for adjournment. At his request, adjourned to come up on 29.01.2026”. Today, brief is held on behalf of learned counsel for the applicant and adjournment is sought without any cogent reason. It appears that the Applicant is not interested in the present proceedings. Accordingly, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported in PLD 2020 SC 518, the reference applications are dismissed for non-prosecution. Office to place copy of this order in each connected reference application. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of the Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Ashraf
